name: Urban League Of Union County
description_short: Non-profit community service organization providing free financial counseling, housing assistance, and reentry support to underserved populations in Union County, NJ since 1944.
description_long:
The Urban League of Union County (ULUC) is a 501(c)(3) non-profit founded in 1944 and incorporated in 1952, serving as one of 90 affiliates of the National Urban League headquartered in New York City. With 80+ years of community presence, the organization is rooted in advancing economic opportunity, equal access, and social justice for African Americans and other marginalized communities across North Jersey. The organization operates from Elizabeth, NJ with three supporting auxiliaries: the Guild-Elizabeth, Guild-Trenton, and Young Professionals chapter.

ULUC offers free and low-cost services spanning education, housing, health, employment, and advocacy. Their housing programs include First-Time Homebuyer Classes and Housing & Community Development initiatives. Employment-focused services include a Youth Entrepreneurship Program and career/internship exploration. They operate a Reentry Program for formerly incarcerated individuals, a Mental Health initiative called "Flip Da Script," and conduct Union County Expungement Clinics providing legal support for record clearance. The organization also hosts voter registration drives and community engagement events.

What distinguishes ULUC is its 80-year track record as an established community institution with deep roots in Union County, multi-program approach addressing interconnected barriers to financial stability, and integration with the national Urban League network. Recent achievements include a $200K grant partnership with Power Home Remodeling and documented community impact initiatives.

As a non-profit, ULUC provides entirely free services with no commercial products or profit motive. However, the website lacks detailed information about specific financial counseling methodologies, certifications beyond general non-profit status, waitlists or service capacity, or concrete client outcome data. Services appear community-based rather than individualized financial advisory.
pros: Established 501(c)(3) non-profit with 80+ years of community presence and trust in Union County
Free services with no hidden fees or commercial motives — purely mission-driven
Multi-program approach addressing housing, employment, reentry, mental health, and youth development simultaneously
Operates Union County Expungement Clinics providing legal support for record clearance at no cost
Affiliated with national Urban League network of 90 organizations providing access to broader resources
Hosts free First-Time Homebuyer Classes specifically designed for underserved populations
Three community auxiliaries (Guild chapters and Young Professionals) supporting and amplifying programs
cons: Website lacks information about specific credit counseling certifications (e.g., NFCC accreditation, HUD approval) despite free-help category requirements
No details on counselor qualifications, training, or whether services are NFCC-certified or HUD-approved
Limited transparency on client outcomes, success rates, waitlists, or service capacity constraints
Website does not specify whether financial/credit counseling is a core service or secondary offering
No pricing structure or service delivery details (online vs. in-person, appointment availability, response times)
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Union County seeking free down payment and mortgage guidance
Formerly incarcerated individuals in North Jersey needing reentry support and expungement assistance
Underserved African American and marginalized community members seeking economic empowerment resources
Youth and young adults interested in entrepreneurship training and career development
diagnosis: ULUC is best for underserved Union County residents—particularly formerly incarcerated individuals, first-time homebuyers, and youth—seeking free community services and advocacy from an established non-profit. Primary caveat: The website does not confirm whether traditional credit counseling or financial literacy is a primary service offering, nor does it document NFCC certification or HUD approval, which are typical markers of free-help organizations in the consumer finance space.
services: Reentry Program for formerly incarcerated individuals
Union County Expungement Clinics (legal record clearance support)
Mental Health initiative — Flip Da Script
Health advocacy and community health services
Equal Opportunity Day Gala and community events
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 1139 E Jersey St Ste 515, Elizabeth, NJ 07201
